stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Maimonides was a Sephardic Jewish philosopher and physician.
  • His book The Guide for the Perplexed reconciled religious and philosophical thought of his era.
  • It was published in 1190 CE.
  • It influenced Jewish, Christian, and Islamic scholars for centuries.
stem: The student wants to identify the year that The Guide for the Perplexed was published. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: A Sephardic Jewish philosopher and physician wrote a major work called The Guide for the Perplexed.
The Guide for the Perplexed was influential for Jewish, Christian, and Islamic scholars for centuries.
Maimonides reconciled religious and philosophical thought in his book The Guide for the Perplexed.
Maimonides's book The Guide for the Perplexed was published in 1190 CE.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence identifies the year in which the book was published: 1190 CE.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the book's author, it doesn't identify the year in which the book was published. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the book's historical influence, it doesn't identify the year in which the book was published. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about the book's contents, it doesn't identify the year in which the book was published.
